mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, labor market tightness supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.0% in more than 50% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances (RSAFS) combined with stable industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy aggregate demand without supply bottlenecks, leading to positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60d returns in more than 60% of cases, or partial correlation with INDPRO alone explains over 80% of the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase discount rates and compress equity multiples, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of tightening episodes, or FEDFUNDS changes show no significant correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive or neutral when HOUST drops below 1200 in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and real GDP (GDPC1) indicate broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or PAYEMS alone explains over 80% of the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects improving supply chain conditions and capacity utilization, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns show no significant positive relationship with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rates exceed 4.5% while unemployment remains below 4.5%, the economy experiences stagflationary pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.
